model_narrative: A pipe network must be laid out to collect production from a set of nodes and route it to a single port. For each directed connection between two nodes the model decides whether to lay a baseline pipe and, on top of that baseline, whether to add a single capacity upgrade. It also decides how much flow runs along each connection. The goal is to choose the cheapest set of pipes and upgrades that can carry every node's production to the port, where the cost of any pipe scales with the distance it spans.

sets.name: k
sets.doc: pipe types; type 2 is the baseline and the rest are larger

sets.name: kk
sets.doc: upgrade pipe types, that is the pipe types larger than the baseline

sets.name: port
sets.doc: the sink node that absorbs all collected production

sets.name: regnode
sets.doc: flow-conserving nodes, that is every node except the port

sets.name: nw
sets.doc: nodes that may originate a pipe

sets.name: arc
sets.doc: directed arcs (i, j); both orientations of every physical edge are present

params.name: cap
params.doc: incremental flow capacity of a pipe type, measured as the extra capacity it adds over the baseline; the baseline type itself carries the baseline capacity

params.name: pipecost
params.doc: incremental cost per unit distance of a pipe type, measured as the extra cost it adds over the baseline

params.name: cap1
params.doc: the baseline pipe capacity that any built pipe provides

params.name: pipecost1
params.doc: the baseline pipe cost per unit distance

params.name: p
params.doc: production at a node; a node with zero production is a pass-through node, a node with positive production is a source

params.name: dist
params.doc: length of an arc between two nodes

vars.name: b
vars.doc: 1 if a baseline pipe is laid on the arc, else 0

vars.name: bk
vars.doc: 1 if the given upgrade type is added on top of the baseline pipe on the arc, else 0

vars.name: cost
vars.doc: total installation cost of the chosen network

constraints.name: cost
constraints.intent: Define the total installation cost as the sum over every arc that starts at a node able to originate a pipe of the arc's length multiplied by the cost of whatever is built on it, where a built baseline pipe contributes its baseline cost and each added upgrade contributes its own incremental cost.

constraints.name: p[i])
constraints.intent: At each pass-through node that has no production, allow at most one baseline pipe to leave the node. Skip this requirement at any node that has production and at any node with no outgoing arcs.

constraints.name: p[i])
constraints.intent: At each node that has production, require exactly one baseline pipe to leave the node so its output has a single starting direction. Skip this requirement at any node with no production and at any node with no outgoing arcs.

constraints.name: regnode:
constraints.intent: At every flow-conserving node, the node's own production must equal the total flow leaving it minus the total flow entering it, so production plus whatever arrives is exactly what departs. Skip this at the port, which simply absorbs all the collected production.

constraints.name: nw:
constraints.intent: On every arc leaving a node that can originate a pipe, the capacity installed there must be enough to carry the flow on that arc, where the installed capacity is the baseline capacity of a built pipe plus the extra capacity contributed by any upgrade added to it.

constraints.name: nw:
constraints.intent: On every arc leaving a node that can originate a pipe, an upgrade may only be added where a baseline pipe is actually built, and since only one upgrade can ever be present the total number of upgrades on the arc can be at most one when a baseline pipe is laid and none otherwise.

query: Permitting is a headache, so we're looking at a leaner layout that lays no more than 28 pipe segments in total. What does the network cost come to under that cap?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_pipes = Constraint(expr=sum(model.b[a] for a in model.arc) <= 28)

query: Why fuss over sizing each segment individually? Just slap the biggest pipe type on every arc we build and be done with it. How much does that one-size-fits-all plan overspend versus the optimized design?
expected_pyomo:
model.heur6 = Constraint(model.arc, rule=lambda m, i, j: m.bk[i, j, '6'] == m.b[i, j])
model.heur_others = Constraint(model.arc, model.kk, rule=lambda m, i, j, k: Constraint.Skip if k == '6' else m.bk[i, j, k] == 0)
